content.full_text:
State of California 
Memoran 
To 
: Advice File 
Date 
Nov. 14, 1980 
From 
fAIR POUTICAl PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Sarah Cameron 
Subject: Investments "wholly or partially" acquired -- Section 87206 
and Section 7(E) of the standard Code, 2 Cal. Adm. Code 
Section 18730(b) (7) (E) 
At the opinion request meeting of November 13, the staff 
considered the application of the provision that requires the 
reporting of the date an investment was "wholly or partially" 
acquired or disposed as it applies to investIT'ents, particularly 
publically traded stock. 
The staff agreed that although there 
may be good reason to know the date of partial acquisition or 
disposal of real property, and perhaps of privately traded 
stock, there is no good reason for such disclosure with respect 
to stock bought and sold over the counter at uhe Stock Exchange. 
If the asset is wholly acquired or disposed, the date is 
important, as it will indicate if the asset was held at the time 
a decision was made which could have affected it. 
As disauali-
fication is required whether the asset is worth $1,001 or 
$1 million, partial acquisition has little import, and partial 
disposal is at best an indication that income was received. 
Pursuant to Reg. 18729, however, no reportable income is 
generated by selling stock through the Stock Exchange. 
We also concluded, however, that there was no way to say 
that some partial acquisitions and disposals were excluded from 
these Sections. 
Consequently, we decided that people in this 
situation were required to report something. 
We ultimately 
decided that disclosure would comport with this requirement if 
it indicated that there were partial acquisitions and disposals 
throughout the reporting period. 
Finally, we decided to put SOIT'e 
sort of amendment on our legislative agenda to remove the 
requirement that partial disposals and acquisitions be reported 
with respect to publically traded stocks. 
c. Bob Stern

sections.question_synthetic: How should the requirement to report the date of investments 'wholly or partially' acquired or disposed be applied to publicly traded stocks under Section 87206 and the standard Code?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that while partial acquisitions and disposals of publicly traded stocks must be reported, disclosure satisfies the requirement if it indicates that such transactions occurred throughout the reporting period, and the staff recommended pursuing a legislative amendment to remove this reporting requirement for publicly traded stocks.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Memorandum format rather than traditional advice letter with Q/C sections. No formal question or conclusion sections present; content extracted from body text. Some OCR errors noted ('Memoran' instead of 'Memorandum', 'investIT'ents' instead of 'investments', 'disauali-fication' instead of 'disqualification'). Document represents staff opinion reached at November 13 meeting, authored by Sarah Cameron.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: How should the requirement to report the date of investments 'wholly or partially' acquired or disposed be applied to publicly traded stocks under Section 87206 and the standard Code?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC concluded that while partial acquisitions and disposals of publicly traded stocks must be reported, disclosure satisfies the requirement if it indicates that such transactions occurred throughout the reporting period, and the staff recommended pursuing a legislative amendment to remove this reporting requirement for publicly traded stocks.

Adm. Code Section 18730(b) (7) (E) At the opinion request meeting of November 13, the staff considered the application of the provision that requires the reporting of the date an investment was "wholly or partially" acquired or disposed as it applies to investIT'ents, particularly publically traded stock. The staff agreed that although there may be good reason to know the date of partial acquisition or disposal of real property, and perhaps of privately traded stock, there is no good reason for such disclosure with respect to stock bought and sold over the counter at uhe Stock Exchange. If the asset is wholly acquired or disposed, the date is important, as it will indicate if the asset was held at the time a decision was made which could have affected it. As disauali- fication is required whether the asset is worth $1,001 or $1 million, partial acquisition has little import, and partial disposal is at best an indication that income was received. Pursuant to Reg. 18729, however, no reportable income is generated by selling stock through the Stock Exchange. We also concluded, however, that there was no way to say that some partial acquisitions and disposals were excluded from these Sections. Consequently, we decided that people in this situation were required to report something. We ultimately decided that disclosure would comport with this requirement if it indicated that there were partial acquisitions and disposals throughout the reporting period. Finally, we decided to put SOIT'e sort of amendment on our legislative agenda to remove the requirement that partial disposals and acquisitions be reported with respect to publically traded stocks. c. Bob Stern
embedding.summary: FPPC staff memorandum addressing the reporting requirements for partial acquisitions and disposals of publicly traded stocks, concluding that disclosure of partial transactions is required but can be satisfied with a general indication of such activity throughout the reporting period.
